sections.title: 2022 AMENDMENT
sections.text: The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.

sections.citation: b067a399-bb9d-4e95-9a75-4a3d37225470, PAGE LOCATION TEXT IN PREVIOUS VERSION  TEXT IN AMENDED VERSION, p. 2
sections.text: 4 Section 7.3 Labelling of Non-Retail Containers Information, as appropriate needed for labelling of retail containers is given either on the nonretail container or in accompanying documents except that the name of the food, date marking and storage instructions, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the nonretail container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to canned beef products designated as "Corned Beef" and sold in hermetically sealed containers which have been heat treated after sealing to such an extent that the product is shelf-stable. It does not apply to meat products of the type "Corned Beef" with compositional characteristics different from those specified. These products shall be designated with a qualifying statement which describes the true nature in such a way that it does not mislead the consumer and that it does not lead to confusion with products covered by this standard.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This standard applies to canned beef products designated as "Corned Beef" and sold in hermetically sealed containers which have been heat treated after sealing to such an extent that the product is shelf-stable. It does not apply to meat products of the type "Corned Beef" with compositional characteristics different from those specified. These products shall be designated with a qualifying statement which describes the true nature in such a way that it does not mislead the consumer and that it does not lead to confusion with products covered by this standard.

page_spans.text: This standard applies to canned beef products designated as "Corned Beef" and sold in hermetically sealed containers which have been heat treated after sealing to such an extent that the product is shelf-stable. It does not apply to meat products of the type "Corned Beef" with compositional characteristics different from those specified. These products shall be designated with a qualifying statement which describes the true nature in such a way that it does not mislead the consumer and that it does not lead to confusion with products covered by this standard.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Corned beef is chopped, cured, boneless carcase meat from animals of bovine species and may include head meat, heart meat and skirt meat. The product shall be prepared from coarsely cut beef which has been precooked or a mixture of such precooked beef to which a maximum of 5% raw beef has been added; in either case, the meat shall be cured before or after filling into the container. The heat treatment shall be applied after the container is sealed and shall be sufficient to ensure that the product is shelf-stable and that it presents no public health hazard.

The heat treatment shall be applied after the container is sealed and shall be sufficient to ensure that the product is shelf-stable and that it presents no public health hazard.

page_spans.text: Corned beef is chopped, cured, boneless carcase meat from animals of bovine species and may include head meat, heart meat and skirt meat. The product shall be prepared from coarsely cut beef which has been precooked or a mixture of such precooked beef to which a maximum of 5% raw beef has been added; in either case, the meat shall be cured before or after filling into the container. The heat treatment shall be applied after the container is sealed and shall be sufficient to ensure that the product is shelf-stable and that it presents no public health hazard.

sections.title: ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
number=3
level=1

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Preservatives used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 08.3.2 “Heat-treated processed comminuted meat, poultry, and game products” and its parent food categories are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Only certain Table 3 food additives (as indicated in Table 3) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Section 4.1 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995), referring to the conditions applying to carry-over of food additives from ingredients and raw materials into foods, shall apply.
sections.retrieval_text:
FOOD ADDITIVES
number=4
level=1
Preservatives used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 08.3.2 “Heat-treated processed comminuted meat, poultry, and game products” and its parent food categories are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Only certain Table 3 food additives (as indicated in Table 3) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Section 4.1 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995), referring to the conditions applying to carry-over of food additives from ingredients and raw materials into foods, shall apply.

page_spans.text: Preservatives used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 08.3.2 “Heat-treated processed comminuted meat, poultry, and game products” and its parent food categories are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Only certain Table 3 food additives (as indicated in Table 3) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Section 4.1 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995), referring to the conditions applying to carry-over of food additives from ingredients and raw materials into foods, shall apply.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides and/or veterinary drugs established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides and/or veterinary drugs established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: Subsidiary Definition
sections.text: Hermetically sealed container means a container which is completely sealed and impermeable and which is made of any appropriate material which is suitable for the product covered by the standard.

sections.title: Essential Ingredients
sections.text: - Uncured beef; - Curing ingredients consisting of food-grade salt and sodium or potassium nitrite.

sections.title: Composition
sections.text: The total protein content in the final product shall not be less than 21% m/m.

sections.title: Raw Material
sections.text: The meat from which the product is prepared shall be of a quality suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours and flavours.
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS > Essential Quality Factors > Raw Material
number=3.4.1
level=3
The meat from which the product is prepared shall be of a quality suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours and flavours.

page_spans.text: The meat from which the product is prepared shall be of a quality suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours and flavours.

sections.title: Final Products
sections.text: The final product shall be clean and substantially free from staining and contamination from the container. The meat shall be uniformly and thoroughly cured and the product shall be capable of being sliced, when chilled.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Meat (CXC 58-2005), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Acid and Acidified Low-Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23-1979), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text: The provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), excepting Section 5.2 (Irradiated Foods) which is not relevant, shall apply:

sections.title: METHODS OF ANALYSIS
sections.text: For checking the compliance with this Standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this Standard shall be used.

sentences.original: The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

sentences.original: Hermetically sealed container means a container which is completely sealed and impermeable and which is made of any appropriate material which is suitable for the product covered by the standard.

prefilter.reason: Standard definition likely relevant to regulatory context.

classification.sentence: Hermetically sealed container means a container which is completely sealed and impermeable and which is made of any appropriate material which is suitable for the product covered by the standard.
classification.pattern: Declarative nominal definition with descriptive clauses

definition: made of any appropriate material suitable for the product

classification.justification: Definition sentence describing a technical term; does not belong to normative obligation, permission or condition modals; no existing template match.

sentences.original: - Uncured beef; - Curing ingredients consisting of food-grade salt and sodium or potassium nitrite.

sentences.original: The total protein content in the final product shall not be less than 21% m/m.

sentences.original: The meat from which the product is prepared shall be of a quality suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours and flavours.

classification.pattern: Conjunctive obligation specifying quality and absence of negative characteristics

classification.justification: Obligation regarding quality and absence of defects; typical normative quality requirement; adapted_template assigned.

sentences.original: The final product shall be clean and substantially free from staining and contamination from the container.

sentences.original: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Meat (CXC 58-2005), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Acid and Acidified Low-Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23-1979), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.

sentences.original: The provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), excepting Section 5.2 (Irradiated Foods) which is not relevant, shall apply:

classification.function: Normative external standard application with exclusion

classification.justification: This sentence establishes binding application of an external standard, excluding one section; typical normative pattern with exception clause; partial match hence adapted_template.

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this Standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this Standard shall be used.

classification.sentence: For checking the compliance with this Standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this Standard shall be used.
classification.pattern: Prepositional phrase + obligation declarative

components.Mandate: methods of analysis and sampling per Recommended Methods (CXS 234-1999)

classification.justification: Specification of mandatory methods for verification; obligation modality with structured reference; fits adapted template for compliance methods.

sentences.original: The name of the food to be declared on the label shall be "Corned Beef".

classification.sentence: The name of the food to be declared on the label shall be "Corned Beef".
classification.pattern: Subject + shall + be + "Literal Label Content"

components.subject: The name of the food to be declared on the label

classification.justification: Sentence expresses a clear obligation with a prescriptive labeling requirement using the structure 'subject shall be "literal text"'. No known template match, new pattern inferred.

sentences.original: For canned corned beef which is a shelf-stable product the date of minimum durability shall be indicated by the year.

classification.sentence: For canned corned beef which is a shelf-stable product the date of minimum durability shall be indicated by the year.
classification.pattern: Condition Clause + subject + shall + be + passive phrase + by + time unit

components.condition: For canned corned beef which is a shelf-stable product
components.subject: the date of minimum durability

classification.justification: The sentence combines a conditional clause with an obligation about labelling date format, without matching existing templates. A new pattern is recognized reflecting conditional obligation with temporal detail.

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

pages.summary: Página 1 contém apenas o título do documento como cabeçalho repetitivo; não há seções ou texto estruturado relevante para hierarquia.

A página contém dois títulos principais em caixa alta sem numeração, representando níveis 1. O primeiro é '2022 AMENDMENT' com texto explicativo sobre a emenda realizada. O segundo é uma linha longa que inicia item detalhado sobre texto anterior versus versão emendada, funcionando como segunda seção principal. Ambos os nós não dão continuidade a outras páginas.
Página com 5 seções principais numeradas, incluindo subseções detalhadas em 2.1, 3.1 a 3.4.2 conforme as numerações apresentadas, refletindo a estrutura formal do documento para RAG.

pages.title: CXS 88-1981
pages.summary: Página com seções 6 a 8, mapando 3 seções principais e 3 subseções sob LABELLING, texto interno associado corretamente, sem indicações de continuidade entre páginas.
